{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Do you want the strengthening to be (A) sentence-level polish and tone tightening only, leaving the core claims as stated, or (B) a substantive reframing that revises the contribution and conclusions to align strictly with the reported error rates (which may involve softening or redirecting claims)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely requires reframing the central contribution and gap to better position the work given the presented results.",
    "R1.2": "The argument chain about why role specialization improves reliability should be checked and tightened so premises and conclusions cohere.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about improved error rates must be aligned precisely with the reported false-positive and false-negative metrics.",
    "RX.1": "Where evidence is mixed, clearly signaling uncertainty strengthens credibility without overstating results.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overclaiming is critical, especially in biomedical contexts where conclusions must not exceed what the data support.",
    "RX.3": "Adhering to the scope ensures the strengthening effort does not introduce new claims or tasks beyond the provided text.",
    "RX.4": "Careful language reduces the risk of downstream clinical misinterpretation or harm stemming from exaggerated performance claims.",
    "RX.5": "Maintaining traceability between statements and reported numbers preserves provenance and enables auditability of any revised claims."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want the strengthening to be (A) sentence-level polish and tone tightening only, leaving the core claims as stated, or (B) a substantive reframing that revises the contribution and conclusions to align strictly with the reported error rates (which may involve softening or redirecting claims)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely requires reframing the central contribution and gap to better position the work given the presented results.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain about why role specialization improves reliability should be checked and tightened so premises and conclusions cohere.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about improved error rates must be aligned precisely with the reported false-positive and false-negative metrics.
rationale.RX.1: Where evidence is mixed, clearly signaling uncertainty strengthens credibility without overstating results.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaiming is critical, especially in biomedical contexts where conclusions must not exceed what the data support.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the scope ensures the strengthening effort does not introduce new claims or tasks beyond the provided text.
rationale.RX.4: Careful language reduces the risk of downstream clinical misinterpretation or harm stemming from exaggerated performance claims.
rationale.RX.5: Maintaining traceability between statements and reported numbers preserves provenance and enables auditability of any revised claims.
